question: A <custom> is...
options: a style of clothing worn for special occasions
a popular dish served at family gatherings
a traditional way of doing things in a culture
a musical instrument played at celebrations
correct: a traditional way of doing things in a culture
explanation: A <custom> is a traditional practice or behavior in a culture.

question: What is <etiquette>?
options: A competitive team sport played outdoors
Rules and conventions of polite behavior
A classical style of playing instruments
A traditional method of preparing food
correct: Rules and conventions of polite behavior
explanation: <Etiquette> refers to the rules and conventions of polite behavior in society.

question: When you <celebrate>, you...
options: clean the house and organize your things
feel disappointed about something that happened
go to work and complete your daily tasks
have a party or special event for something good
correct: have a party or special event for something good
explanation: To <celebrate> means to do something special to mark a happy occasion.

question: A <host> is someone who...
options: organizes an event and welcomes guests
delivers packages and letters to homes
prepares all the food for the event
cleans the house before a big party
correct: organizes an event and welcomes guests
explanation: A <host> is the person who organizes an event and welcomes guests.

question: You need a <prescription> to...
options: travel abroad to a different country
buy certain medicines from the pharmacy
open a new bank account at a branch
enter a government building or office
correct: buy certain medicines from the pharmacy
explanation: A <prescription> is a doctor's written order for medicine.

question: If you have a <fever>, your body temperature is...
options: higher than the normal level for humans
lower than what is considered healthy
exactly at the standard healthy level
changing constantly throughout the day
correct: higher than the normal level for humans
explanation: A <fever> means your body temperature is above the normal 37°C / 98.6°F.

question: A <check-up> is...
options: a routine visit to the doctor for your health
a method of payment used at some stores
a written evaluation given at the school
a competitive event between sports teams
correct: a routine visit to the doctor for your health
explanation: A <check-up> is a regular visit to the doctor to make sure you are healthy.

question: A <browser> is used to...
options: access and view websites on the internet
take and edit photographs on your phone
send and receive short text messages
make voice and video calls to friends
correct: access and view websites on the internet
explanation: A <browser> is software like Chrome or Firefox used to view websites.

question: You need <Wi-Fi> to...
options: charge your phone battery at full speed
make a phone call to another country
connect to the internet without any cables
listen to the radio on a portable device
correct: connect to the internet without any cables
explanation: <Wi-Fi> is a wireless technology that allows devices to connect to the internet.

question: A <tradition> is...
options: a type of technology used in daily life
a kind of medicine prescribed by doctors
something people do for many years in their culture
a recently created invention or modern device
correct: something people do for many years in their culture
explanation: A <tradition> is a custom or belief passed down through generations.

question: A <symptom> is...
options: a special tool that doctors use at work
a type of medicine you take every day
a physical sign that you might be sick
a private room inside a large hospital
correct: a physical sign that you might be sick
explanation: A <symptom> is a physical sign that indicates illness, like a cough or fever.

question: You take <medicine> when you...
options: are hungry and want to eat something
want to exercise and stay in good shape
are sick and need to feel better soon
need to study for an important exam
correct: are sick and need to feel better soon
explanation: <Medicine> is a substance used to treat illness and help you recover.

question: An <appointment> is...
options: a type of holiday celebrated in many countries
an unexpected surprise visit from a close friend
a random event that happens without any planning
a scheduled meeting at a specific time and place
correct: a scheduled meeting at a specific time and place
explanation: An <appointment> is a planned meeting, often with a doctor or professional.

question: To <download> means to...
options: permanently delete files stored on your device
transfer files from the internet to your device
print a document using a connected printer
send important files to someone by email
correct: transfer files from the internet to your device
explanation: To <download> means to copy data from the internet to your computer or phone.

question: A <password> is used to...
options: find your phone when you lose it somewhere
protect your accounts and personal information
remember your name and personal details easily
pay for things you buy at stores or online
correct: protect your accounts and personal information
explanation: A <password> is a secret word or phrase used to access protected accounts.

question: To <upload> means to...
options: delete files that you no longer need at all
send files from your device to the internet
download files from a website to your phone
copy files to a USB drive for safe storage
correct: send files from your device to the internet
explanation: To <upload> means to transfer files from your device to a website or cloud.

question: A <ceremony> is...
options: a formal event with special traditions and rituals
a casual meeting between friends at a coffee shop
a type of exercise that helps you stay healthy
a daily routine that you follow every single morning
correct: a formal event with special traditions and rituals
explanation: A <ceremony> is a formal event, like a wedding or graduation.

question: If you have an <allergy>, you...
options: feel very happy and excited about something good
react badly to certain things like food or pollen
are very hungry and want to eat a big meal
need glasses to see things that are far away
correct: react badly to certain things like food or pollen
explanation: An <allergy> is a negative physical reaction to certain substances.

question: A <website> is...
options: a type of book you can buy at a store
a TV channel that shows movies and news
a radio station that plays music all day
a page or group of pages on the internet
correct: a page or group of pages on the internet
explanation: A <website> is a collection of web pages accessible through the internet.

question: A <festival> is...
options: a kind of transport used to travel between cities
a type of school where students learn new subjects
a special event with music, food, or cultural activities
a regular workday at the office or at your company
correct: a special event with music, food, or cultural activities
explanation: A <festival> is an organized event celebrating something, often with entertainment.
